justification: MJ recommends 5 services (not 6+) with clear justification tied to the 8-person team constraint. Location Tracking is correctly identified as a separate service due to 5,000 writes/sec requiring a time-series store. Payment is correctly separated for consistency and PCI-adjacent reasons. Strangler fig migration is explicitly recommended with a detailed 4-phase plan. trade_offs includes operational overhead (5 services for 8 engineers) and distributed tracing complexity (monitoring blind spots risk). risks includes distributed transaction failures in the order-payment flow. MJ explicitly advises against over-decomposition ('5 services for 8 engineers is already aggressive') and against big-bang rewrite.

observations.text: Excellent justification for merging Customer Account into Order Service to stay at 5 services, with a clear extraction path if it grows
Phased strangler fig migration is detailed with specific week ranges and rollback criteria
Distributed transaction risk explicitly called out with idempotency keys and reconciliation job as mitigation
Team skill gap acknowledged as a medium-severity risk with a practical mitigation (start with simplest extraction)
Observability positioned as a Phase 0 prerequisite, not an afterthought
Context map with DDD relationship types (Customer-Supplier, Conformist, ACL) shows deep domain modeling
PCI compliance boundary not explicitly mentioned as a reason for Payment separation — only 'strict consistency' is cited
Pitfalls section explicitly warns against creating more than 5 services and against big-bang migration

agent_output_excerpt:
{
  "executive_summary": "Decompose the courier platform monolith into six bounded contexts mapped to five deployable services (merging Customer Account into Order Management as a supporting concern).
